methodology: Platform-product OKRs with explicit customer outcomes and team ownership.
description: Preparation Objectives for the next quarter.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe platform outcomes for internal customers, not internal feature checklists.
Key results emphasize adoption, economics, controls, and reliability.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Scale self-service without scaling platform support work
companyObjectives.statement: Prepare the next quarter around platform leverage: more teams should complete core workflows on their own while enablement load stays bounded.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shapes where self-service should expand and where it should stay narrower.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q2 Make platform value and cost visible enough to govern as a product portfolio
companyObjectives.statement: Prepare the next quarter around platform economics and portfolio clarity so teams can make better investment, adoption, and retirement decisions.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Highlights the need to join flow metrics with cost and usage evidence.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Scale the onboarding paved road across more team archetypes
objectives.objective: Expand the standard onboarding path so more product teams can adopt the platform without bespoke guidance, migration detours, or recurring support dependence.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Measures how quickly teams become productive on the platform.
Share of onboarding and setup flows completed without platform intervention.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of mainstream product teams using the standard platform path.

keyResults.target: Make the onboarding path work for a broader set of mainstream team shapes
keyResults.baseline: Onboarding works for common paths but still slows down on more complex service patterns.
keyResults.statement: Reduce onboarding time for more team archetypes by extending templates, ownership defaults, and diagnostics beyond the simplest service cases.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to broaden onboarding usability across more service shapes.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by expanding supported service templates and migration quality.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve as the platform broadens support for mainstream onboarding scenarios.

keyResults.target: Increase the share of teams that complete setup without platform intervention
keyResults.baseline: More advanced team shapes still require enablement help to complete the platform path.
keyResults.statement: Increase self-service completion by closing the most common onboarding escape hatches that still route teams into manual help.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to eliminate common setup exceptions through more complete default paths.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR with better workflow guidance and task completion visibility.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through evidence-led iteration of the paved road.

objectives.title: 2026-Q2 Govern runtime and delivery as a product portfolio
objectives.objective: Make runtime self-service fast enough and cost-visible enough that teams can choose standard paths deliberately and the platform can retire expensive exceptions.

linkedCustomerKPIs.kpiName: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Elapsed time from a new service starting to its first production release on the platform.
Engineering effort spent keeping custom delivery pipelines working outside the platform standard path.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Time required for a code change to reach production through the platform path.

linkedBusinessKPIs.kpiName: Cost, Capacity and Usage Insights coverage
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Coverage of runtime services with usable cost and usage insight for engineering decisions.

keyResults.metric: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
keyResults.target: Keep shrinking repo-to-production time on the standard runtime path
keyResults.baseline: The standard path is improving but still loses time on more advanced environment and dependency shapes.
keyResults.statement: Reduce time to first production deployment by packaging more runtime prerequisites and rollout patterns into reusable defaults.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to lower repo-to-production time through more complete self-service runtime setup.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR with more reusable release flows.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through continued expansion of the supported runtime path.

keyResults.metric: Cost, Capacity and Usage Insights coverage
keyResults.target: Expand runtime cost and usage visibility enough to govern the platform as a portfolio
keyResults.baseline: Usage and cost signals are still incomplete for many runtime services and exception paths.
keyResults.statement: Increase cost and usage insight coverage so teams and platform owners can see which runtime defaults create leverage and which still hide expensive exceptions.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to make runtime cost, capacity, and usage evidence visible enough for decision making.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR with richer environment and lifecycle signals.
Support the KR with release-usage and exception evidence.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should improve through better instrumentation and clearer platform economics.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows where runtime leverage and runtime cost still need to be balanced more explicitly.
